title: GeoAI Runtime Design Recovery Program — CR 10.03 to CR 10.07

runtimeBodyMigrationAuthorization.scope: Draft branch and Vercel Preview only

executiveFinding: PR #111 released Product System v3.2.1 foundation, shared shell, tokens and primitives while intentionally preserving route bodies through a 20-case invariance contract. The current Production therefore does not represent full Figma screen migration. Runtime recovery must replace body invariance with route-specific Figma parity and product-quality acceptance.
mandatoryCaveat: Screening hypothesis; official validation required; not a legal, cadastral, zoning, planning, engineering, insurance or valuation conclusion.

semanticColorPolicy.brandBlue: Brand identity, shared shell, navigation, information structure and focus boundaries.
semanticColorPolicy.spatialTeal: Map interaction, spatial intelligence, analytical emphasis, evidence-positive states and primary product actions.
semanticColorPolicy.validationAmber: Validation gaps and official confirmation requirements only inside decision and evidence contexts; never as a commercial landing overlay.
semanticColorPolicy.criticalRed: True blocking, failure or material risk states only.
semanticColorPolicy.interactionRule: Hover, pressed and focus states remain inside the same semantic hue family; interaction must not change the meaning of a control.

changeRequests.title: Commercial Landing and Request Access Runtime Alignment
changeRequests.problem: The commercial landing includes a validation strip that overlaps body copy and exposes internal validation language as a primary marketing visual.
changeRequests.businessReason: The public landing must communicate GeoAI value clearly, preserve data honesty and feel production-grade without internal workflow artefacts.
changeRequests.dataImpact: None. Public copy and navigation only; no data transmission or persistence change.
changeRequests.designImpact: Remove overlapping validation overlay, retain commercial hierarchy, preserve approved shared shell and identity, and keep caveats in bounded legal/data-honesty positions.
changeRequests.engineeringImpact: Landing CSS/body correction, regression contract and responsive verification.
risks: Removing all caveats would weaken data honesty
Changing public routes could break request/demo funnels
acceptance: No validation/caution element overlaps commercial copy at desktop, tablet or mobile widths.
The landing retains one meaningful h1 and all current commercial anchors and request/demo routes.
Validation language is absent from process-card overlays and remains available only in a bounded global disclosure.
No horizontal overflow, clipping or text truncation on declared viewports.

changeRequests.title: Workspace Core Screen Migration
changeRequests.problem: Workspace retains shell-only alignment; scenario copy is clamped and a large validation disclosure competes with the primary workflow.
changeRequests.businessReason: The map-first and criteria-first workspace is the core product proof and must match the spatial, evidence-driven Figma composition.
changeRequests.dataImpact: No source, persistence, scoring or authority change. Existing deterministic browser-local behavior is preserved.
changeRequests.designImpact: Preserve map-dominant composition, make scenario text fully readable, reduce validation disclosure to a compact decision-context note, and use spatial teal for analytical emphasis.
changeRequests.engineeringImpact: Scoped workspace presentation layer, route-specific regression tests and Figma-node traceability.
risks: Presentation changes could obscure existing controls
A global CSS fix could unintentionally affect other routes
acceptance: Scenario title and subtitle render without one-line clipping.
The former expandable Validation caveat does not occupy the primary setup card.
Map-first and criteria-first behavior, controls and keyboard flow remain unchanged.
Workspace remains map-dominant on desktop and usable on 390/430/768/834 widths.

changeRequests.title: Decision Intelligence Runtime Migration
changeRequests.problem: Analysis and comparison surfaces are functionally complete but not yet accepted as Figma-parity decision screens.
changeRequests.businessReason: Decision screens must answer what happens, why it matters, risk, impact, next action and evidence in one reviewable hierarchy.
changeRequests.dataImpact: No new claims or live integrations. Existing sample/open and user-provided contexts remain visibly distinguished.
changeRequests.designImpact: One primary takeaway per screen, evidence lineage visible, teal analytical hierarchy, amber only for validation gaps and no unsupported readiness claims.
changeRequests.engineeringImpact: ExpressDashboard, ComparisonDashboard and evidence/source components migrate under route-specific acceptance.
risks: Cosmetic score emphasis could imply certified valuation
Evidence visibility could be reduced by over-simplification
acceptance: Primary decision posture, score, confidence, validation state and next action are visible without scrolling on desktop.
Source lineage and validation gaps remain accessible and honest.
No certified valuation, official zoning, ownership or approved-site implication is introduced.
Analysis and comparison keyboard/Axe flows remain green.

changeRequests.title: Reports and Print Runtime Redesign
changeRequests.problem: The current A4 report collapses grids into single columns under print media, creates sparse pages and presents a schematic fallback as a missing map.
changeRequests.businessReason: Reports are client-facing deliverables and must be executive-grade, evidence-aware and printable without appearing like a raw browser dump.
changeRequests.dataImpact: No new data source. Captured map snapshots remain preferred; fallback remains explicitly schematic and non-official.
changeRequests.designImpact: Restore multi-column A4 composition, create a clear executive summary, make the map the visual anchor, reduce repeated caveats and improve page density.
changeRequests.engineeringImpact: Print media overrides, report component restructuring, improved context map fallback and PDF regression checks.
risks: Fallback visual could be mistaken for an official map
Print pagination can vary by browser
acceptance: A4 print forces three-column metadata and score grids and two-column executive sections regardless of print viewport width.
The first page contains an executive summary and a dominant captured or explicitly schematic site-context map.
No avoidable empty page or browser-layout-induced single-column card stack remains.
Fallback is labelled schematic context and cannot be interpreted as cadastral or official geometry.
Mandatory caveat, source lineage, generation timestamp and report identity remain present.

changeRequests.title: Semantic Color Normalization
changeRequests.problem: Blue and teal/green are used inconsistently across commercial, spatial and analytical contexts, while validation amber is over-prominent in some screens.
changeRequests.businessReason: A stable semantic palette improves comprehension, trust, accessibility and Figma-to-code consistency.
changeRequests.designImpact: Apply the semantic color policy in this program without changing approved foundation tokens.
changeRequests.engineeringImpact: Route-scoped aliases and component usage normalization; no token foundation rewrite.
risks: Changing semantic colors globally could break accessibility or state meaning
acceptance: Brand blue structures the shell and information hierarchy.
Spatial teal leads maps, analytical actions and evidence-positive states.
Amber appears only for validation/caution; red only for blocking/critical states.
Pressed/hover states do not switch semantic hue families.
Contrast and focus-visible evidence remain compliant.
